references.title: An Adaptive Learning Path Builder based on a Context Aware Recommender System

extracted.Courses: 1 Fundamentals to Informatics and 1 Costume Design courses
extracted.Data sources:
Questionnaire, Social Networks, 
Contextual Information,
Professor
extracted.Initialization Method:
Item Profiles are created and labeled by professors nd subsequently vectorized.

Student specific data is extracted from the questionnaire after discretization.
extracted.Updates to Recommendations:
Success in modules is recorded by the Knowledge Base Module after obtaining ratings on a specific module.

If the user does not answer the questionnaire, the ratings are updated via inference.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Users and Items have the same dimension fixed in advance and cannot have negative elements.

Users are clustered using cosine similarity.

Context (Location and Day of Week) are added in the Knowledeg-Base Module.
extracted.Recommendation types: Learning Path that addresses student difficulties
extracted.Evaluation:
The test of the system was conducted via survey. Participants reported:

Utility, Reliability, Recommendation, Usability and Performance. These were evaluated in a Likert Scale
extracted.Comments:
In this short paper, the authors create a recommender system framework meant to recommend exercises and materials using “contextual information”.

The system is designed into separate modules:

Data Management & Knowledge Base: The stages at whixh the authors do the data collection and preprocessing.

The RS Moduule and CARS Modules: The RS Module is the main dataflow for the creatioon of the of the PathBuilder. If it fails, then the data goes thrrough CARS which does not include the contextual features. 

Path Builder Module: Does the recommendations.
extracted.Limitations:
Validation via self-reporting.

Small sample size for evaluation of system. 

Unclear form of recommendation.

No assessment of model performance.

annotations.text: There is a big increase in tools and services that now create novel learning pathways for learners. In this scenario, the challenge becomes recommending the correct pathway to the correct student. The paper presents a recommender system that introduces recommendations via multimedia content.
The recommender seems to follow a content-based filtering approach and is tested in 2 courses with different characteristics.
